ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 304, HEARING RE: INTERVENOR THE TRAVELERS INDEMNITY COMPANY (ON BEHALF OF DEFENDANT SUPRO CORPORATION) MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING WAS HEARING REQUIRED. HEARING HELD. AT THE CONCLUSION OF DISCUSSION, THE PARTIES' AGREE THAT COUNSEL FOR TRAVELERS WILL SUBMIT A PLEADING WITHDRAWING INTERVENOR'S ANSWER-IN-INTERVENTION WHICH IS GRANTED BY THE COURT. THE COURT GRANTED THE MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF RECORD AND INSTRUCTED COUNSEL FOR INTERVENORS TO SUBMIT THE ORDER GRANTING THE MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL ON THE MANDATORY JUDICIAL COUNCIL FORM (MC-053). JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: ED SANTOS AND JACKIE ALAMEDA; MATTER NOT REPORTED; BAILIFF: NOT PRESENT. (D304/EPS) **FOLLOWING THE HEARING, A REVIEW OF THE REGISTER OF ACTION REFLECTS THAT AN ANSWER-IN-INTERVENTION WAS NEVER FILED.**

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 502, THE APPLICATION OF PLAINTIFFS DAVID HARMS AND SEPTEMBER FARUZZI-HARMS ("PLAINTIFFS") FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANTS (1) NEWTON ENTERPRISES IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,000,000.00; AND (2) SUPRO CORPORATION IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,000,000.00 IS GRANTED. THE COURT HAS REVIEWED THE PROVE-UP PACKET INCLUDING THE JUDICIAL COUNCIL FORM CIV-100, THE MEMORANDUM, MR. NEVIN'S DECLARATION AND THE ATTACHED EXHIBITS, WHICH ARE ADMITTED. THE COURT FINDS DEFENDANTS HAVE BEEN SERVED WITH PROCESS, FAILED TO ANSWER OR APPEAR AND DEFEND THE ACTION WITHIN THE TIME ALLOWED BY LAW, AND DEFAULT HAS BEEN DULY ENTERED. AS SUCH, PLAINTIFFS ARE ENTITLED TO THE RELIEF SOUGHT IN THE APPLICATION. THE COURT INTENDS TO EXECUTE THE PROPOSED JUDGMENT ACCOMPANYING THE APPLICATION. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; CLERK: JOHNNY SENGMANY; REPORTER: NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-07-2026 Continued To Oct-01-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 502

DEFAULT HEARING (PROVE UP)
Off Calendar May-12-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday May 12, 2026, in Department 304.
No appearance, no opposition.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

The Application of Plaintiffs David Harms and September Faruzzi-Harms ("Plaintiffs") for Entry of Default Judgment against Defendants (1) Newton Enterprises in the amount of $2,000,000.00; and (2) Supro Corporation in the amount of $2,000,000.00 is GRANTED.
The Court has reviewed the prove-up packet including the Judicial Council Form CIV-100, the memorandum, Mr. Nevin's declaration and the attached exhibits, which are admitted. The Court finds Defendants have been served with process, failed to answer or appear and defend the action within the time allowed by law, and default has been duly entered. As such, Plaintiffs are entitled to the relief sought in the Application. The Court intends to execute the proposed judgment accompanying the application. =(305/skf).

DEFAULT HEARING (PROVE UP)
Off Calendar May-06-2026 Continued To May-12-26 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 502

MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar Feb-13-2026 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Oct-01-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 304

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Feb-10-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom: Kimberly Chu, Esq. of Brayton Purcell appearing for plaintiff David Harms; Hanna Thompson, Esq. of Foley Mansfield appearing for intervenor The Travelers Indemnity Company (on behalf of defendant Supro Corp.)

Hearing re: INTERVENOR THE TRAVELERS INDEMNITY COMPANY (ON BEHALF OF DEFENDANT SUPRO CORPORATION) MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD.

The court's tentative ruling was hearing required. Hearing Held. The court and counsel confer re: the withdrawal of Intervenor's Answer-in-Intervention, defendant Supro, the motion to be relieved as counsel, and the upcoming trial date.

At the conclusion of discussion, the parties' agree that counsel for Travelers will submit a pleading withdrawing intervenor's answer-in-intervention which is GRANTED by the court. The court granted the MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF RECORD and instructed counsel for intervenors to submit the order granting the motion to be relieved as counsel on the mandatory judicial council form (MC-053).

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Ed Santos and Jackie Alameda; Matter not reported; Bailiff: Not Present. (D304/EPS)

**following the hearing, a review of the register of action reflects that an answer-in-intervention was never filed.**

MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar Jan-12-2026 Continued To Feb-17-26 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 206

ASBESTOS CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-17-2025 Set For Trial On Jan-12-26 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 206
